title: Uniface 10: Changes in Subtype Property Inheritance
breadcrumbs: What's New in Uniface 10 Compared to Uniface 9
Uniface 10: Changes in Subtype Property Inheritance
text: Uniface 10: Changes in Subtype Property Inheritance In Uniface 10, property inheritance for subtype entities and fields has changed. In Uniface 10, subtype entities can inherit properties from their supertypes. In Uniface 9, this behavior was simulated to a limited extent by copying values when the subtype was created. For most properties, subsequent changes to supertype properties were not inherited. Note: To ensure compatibility, migrated subtypes continue to use the copied property values, with the exception of properties that cannot be specialized on subtypes or in component entities.For more information, see Migration of Subtype Properties . Properties that must be inherited The following properties are always inherited and cannot be specialized in the subtype: For subtype entities: Database Behavior (U_INDB) Database Path (U_DBMS) Database Interface (Entities) (U_INT) For subtype fields: Data Type Database Interface (Entities) (U_INT) Is External Properties that cannot be inherited The following properties cannot be inherited from the supertype because a null value (which normally means the property is inherited) has a special meaning for these properties. For subtype entities: Popup Menu (UPOPUP) DSP Widget Properties (DSP_WIDGETPROPERTIES) FRM Widget Properties (FRM_WIDGETPROPERTIES) USP Widget Properties (USP_WIDGETPROPERTIES) For subtype fields: Initial Value (INITVALUE) Field Layout (DATA_LAYOUT) Field Syntax (DATA_SYNTAX) Value/Representation (VALLAB) Popup Menu (UPOPUP) DSP Widget Properties (DSP_WIDGETPROPERTIES) FRM Widget Properties (FRM_WIDGETPROPERTIES) USP Widget Properties (USP_WIDGETPROPERTIES) Properties that can be inherited All other properties of subtype entities and fields can be inherited from the supertype. Setting or changing the values in the subtype overrides the inherited value, but will be inherited when the subtype is used in a component. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
